patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] btrfs: release extent lock per folio in readahead

version_history.subject: [PATCH] btrfs: release extent lock per folio in readahead

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch modifies the btrfs readahead function to release extent locks per folio, rather than holding them for the entire readahead loop. This change is intended to prevent a race condition between readahead and direct IO writes that was introduced in kernel version 6.14. The patch reverts the behavior of locking/unlocking extents as a unit around the readahead loop, which was changed in kernel version 6.12. By releasing extent locks per folio, overlapping reads can proceed without blocking on the extent lock.

review_comments.author: Qu Wenruo
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Qu Wenruo noted that the patch does not address a potential issue where compressed readahead can expand the RA range, resulting in folios without extent locks, but assured that direct reads on compressed extents will always fallback to buffered reads and thus there is no window to race with direct reads.
review_comments.raw_body:
Looks good to me.

Reviewed-by: Qu Wenruo <wqu@suse.com>


Just to mention that Sashiko gave a review with level high, which 
doesn't seem to be caused by this patch itself, and seems to be a false 
alert.

Btrfs compressed readahead can expand the RA range, resulting folios 
that has no extent lock at all.
Thus in theory we still can have some folios without extent lock for 
compressed readahead.

But thankfully direct read on compressed extent will always fallback to 
buffered read, thus there is no window to race with direct reads.

Thanks,
Qu

review_comments.author: Qu Wenruo
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Qu Wenruo noted that unlocking the extent lock for a folio in readahead can still cause issues if other threads are holding locks on overlapping ranges, and suggested adding an extra check to ensure the unlock is only done within the original readahead range.
review_comments.raw_body:
Wait, this is still problematic, that we can clear the EXTENT_LOCK for 
ranges that is held by other threads.

The read itself is protected by folio lock so we won't race, but some 
other readers may got their extent lock unlocked still seems problematic.

Can we do an extra check on the folio range and only unlock if it's 
inside the original RA range?

Thanks,
Qu

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] btrfs: fallback to transaction csum tree on a commit root csum miss

version_history.subject: [PATCH] btrfs: fallback to transaction csum tree on a commit root csum miss

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch introduces a fallback mechanism to the transaction csum tree when there's a commit root csum miss in btrfs. It addresses an issue where relocation operations don't update the extent generation, leading to zero csum errors. The patch adds a generic fallback to try again with the transaction csum root after a relocation transaction.

review_comments.author: Filipe Manana
review_comments.summary: Identified a minor syntax error in the patch code and provided a Reviewed-by tag.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Thu, Jun 11, 2026 at 11:04 PM Boris Burkov <boris@bur.io> wrote:
>
> We have been running with commit root csums enabled for some time and
> have noticed a slight uptick in zero csum errors. Investigating those
> revealed that they were same transaction reads of extents that were just
> relocated, but the extent map generation was long ago.
>
> It turns out that relocation intentionally does not update the extent
> generation (replace_file_extents(), but must write a new csum since the

There's a missing closing parenthesis here.

> data has moved, so we must account for this with commit root csum reading.
>
> Luckily this is a short lived condition: after the relocation transaction
> the commit root will once again have the csum. So we can add a generic
> fallback to the lookup to try again with the transaction csum root.
>
> Fixes: f07b855c56b1 ("btrfs: try to search for data csums in commit root")
> Signed-off-by: Boris Burkov <boris@bur.io>

Reviewed-by: Filipe Manana <fdmanana@suse.com>

Thanks.

> ---
>  fs/btrfs/file-item.c | 26 +++++++++++++++++++++++++-
>  1 file changed, 25 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
>
> diff --git a/fs/btrfs/file-item.c b/fs/btrfs/file-item.c
> index 9f6454e9db818..cf50fd623f41a 100644
> --- a/fs/btrfs/file-item.c
> +++ b/fs/btrfs/file-item.c
> @@ -358,6 +358,7 @@ int btrfs_lookup_bio_sums(struct btrfs_bio *bbio)
>         const unsigned int nblocks = orig_len >> fs_info->sectorsize_bits;
>         int ret = 0;
>         u32 bio_offset = 0;
> +       bool using_commit_root = false;
>
>         if ((inode->flags & BTRFS_INODE_NODATASUM) ||
>             test_bit(BTRFS_FS_STATE_NO_DATA_CSUMS, &fs_info->fs_state))
> @@ -431,6 +432,7 @@ int btrfs_lookup_bio_sums(struct btrfs_bio *bbio)
>          * from across transactions.
>          */
>         if (bbio->csum_search_commit_root) {
> +               using_commit_root = true;
>                 path->search_commit_root = true;
>                 path->skip_locking = true;
>                 down_read(&fs_info->commit_root_sem);
> @@ -463,6 +465,28 @@ int btrfs_lookup_bio_sums(struct btrfs_bio *bbio)
>                  * assume this is the case.
>                  */
>                 if (count == 0) {
> +                       /*
> +                        * If an extent is relocated in the current transaction
> +                        * then relocation writes a new csum without updating
> +                        * the extent map generation. Until the next commit, we
> +                        * will see a hole in that case, so we need to fallback
> +                        * to searching the transaction csum root.
> +                        *
> +                        * Note that a commit root lookup of a referenced extent can
> +                        * only miss, not return a stale csum. A freed extent's csum
> +                        * is deleted in the same transaction and its bytenr is not
> +                        * reusable until that transaction has committed and the
> +                        * extent is unpinned.
> +                        */
> +                       if (using_commit_root) {
> +                               up_read(&fs_info->commit_root_sem);
> +                               using_commit_root = false;
> +                               path->search_commit_root = false;
> +                               path->skip_locking = false;
> +                               btrfs_release_path(path);
> +                               continue;
> +                       }
> +
>                         memset(csum_dst, 0, csum_size);
>                         count = 1;
>
> @@ -481,7 +505,7 @@ int btrfs_lookup_bio_sums(struct btrfs_bio *bbio)
>                 bio_offset += count * sectorsize;
>         }
>
> -       if (bbio->csum_search_commit_root)
> +       if (using_commit_root)
>                 up_read(&fs_info->commit_root_sem);
>         return ret;
>  }
> --
> 2.52.0
>
>

patches_acked.subject: Re: [PATCH 0/2] btrfs: fix incorrect barrier usage in relocation
